1fZXJqN5G1wFv17o5jCe5z48X-wazvjL79k0y08y.title: Weekly Product Update
1fZXJqN5G1wFv17o5jCe5z48X-wazvjL79k0y08y.summary:
Client communication issues drove discussion for new processes before reviewing the Impetus One rollout plan, which included a pilot launch and full migration details.

Improve Client Communication Process
Frustration over a lack of client communication following issue resolution necessitated a new process to centralize and follow through on all client requests and concerns. All communication and work completion must be documented to apply to all clients, including those handled by Webugol.

Review Impetus One Rollout
The high-level Impetus One rollout plan was presented, detailing Phase 1's pilot launch for Valhalla Vitality focusing on operational stability and core connectivity. Phase 2 targets system consolidation, including full e-commerce integration and the migration of the legacy Impetus RX system by June.

Define Data Flow Strategy
A critical distinction was established for data roles, confirming that all medical intake forms and data must strictly reside in Impetus One, not HubSpot or Medusa. It was agreed that Impetus One should trigger web hooks to HubSpot for patient communications and email automations.




Next steps
* [Rochelle Blaza] Centralize Communications: Reply to client email addressing recent issues. Ensure Shahad submits all requests and concerns to the new centralized HR email.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Coordinate Webugol Process: Meet with Eugene regarding the client communication process. Ensure implementation covers all Webugol outsourced work.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Identify Marketing Clients: Talk to Eugene about marketing clients. Determine service scopes and size all outstanding requests.
* [Rochelle Blaza, Christian Leonard Roman] Define Client Process: Develop formal client communication process for managing small requests. Plan to present new documented process at the next update meeting.
* [Christian Leonard Roman] Revise Project Plan: Update project plan document reflecting Phase 1 excludes e-commerce integration. Revise Phase 4 migration scope to include patient data from Impetus RX and Practice Sync.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Create Shabazz Tickets: Create task tickets for Shahbaz detailing e-commerce work. Include loyalty program, subscription module, and Medusa to Impetus One integration.
* [Philip D'Agostino] Review Medusa Work: Review Shahbaz progress on Medusa development. Ensure complete coverage of project requirements post-Aluja offboarding later this week or next week.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Evaluate Forms Module: Investigate the form builder functionality within the Impetus One platform.
* [Christian Leonard Roman] Architect Data Flow: Design data flow architecture for registration, purchase, and syncing. Cover all components including Medusa, Impetus One, and CRM systems.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Coordinate Valhalla Expectations: Connect with Valhalla Vitality team members (Konrad, Rosanna, Olesia). Gain clarity on specific HubSpot usage, required integrations, and current automation platform status.
* [Philip D'Agostino] Discuss HubSpot Implementation: Discuss HubSpot implementation and necessary automation requirements with Olesia.
* [Philip D'Agostino] Address Staffing Needs: Call Mazen after this meeting to discuss critical staffing needs. Highlight the necessity of hiring dedicated Valhalla Vitality personnel.

1f7faxewb3YcN61bh8oHuE23JbgKHxWmicuLPceC.title: Software Brainstorm & Prototype
1f7faxewb3YcN61bh8oHuE23JbgKHxWmicuLPceC.summary:
Discussions evaluated software prototypes and pyramid distribution models while aligning on critical payment and shipping protocols.

System Demonstration and Comparison
Participants evaluated the Impetus One prototype and compared its features against the existing Blend Care system. The review highlighted essential functionalities like inventory management and sales commission tracking.

Platform Scalability and Features
Discussions focused on building a pyramid distribution model and integrating necessary sales marketing tools. It was decided that Impetus One must incorporate advanced commission and hierarchy management features.

Payment and Shipping Protocols
The group established that all medical orders require upfront payment to mitigate financial risk. Shipping is restricted to weekdays to ensure cold-chain integrity and prevent product damage.




Next steps
* [Gary Poole] Secure Payment: Secure a payment processor and merchant account for the new organization, leveraging Anthony's help.
* [Philip D'Agostino] Develop Layer: Build the additional abstraction layer into the system required to accommodate multiple sales reps and business units.
* [Gary Poole] Coordinate Onboarding: Contact Anthony immediately; request the full product list, including prices available for shifting clients; emphasize the urgent requirement for the merchant account setup.
* [Philip D'Agostino] Share Access: Provide Gary Poole with temporary login credentials to the software for testing and trial use.
* [Gary Poole] Complete Setup: Meet with Anthony and Jessica to finalize system setup. Discuss activation of the merchant account to allow order placement next week.
* [Philip D'Agostino] Noodle Layers: Contemplate discussed complexities regarding the business layers.

1O2crGZlU4mDm4xcFGKwkt8o0aI4er1yH0Ys5-tM.title: eCommerce Migration Weekly Update
1O2crGZlU4mDm4xcFGKwkt8o0aI4er1yH0Ys5-tM.summary:
Front-end and back-end integration issues with product rendering were reviewed, with a final decision made regarding user status logic and medical intake data flow.

Registration Flow Functional
The demonstrated registration and login flow using email and a temporary code is fully functional, requiring only styling updates for the form and integration of a user-friendly date picker library.

Product Rendering Issues
Product rendering is currently failing on the staging environment, though it works locally, which is suspected to be due to data synchronization issues between Medusa and the database. A PR will be merged to resynchronize all Medusa products, making fresh data available by the following morning.

User Status Logic Determined
User status logic was defined, classifying registered users without a filled medical intake as users, not patients, which dictates access restrictions to products in Medusa. The team decided that medical intake data will be sent directly to Impetus One via N8N, with only a check mark sent to HubSpot.




Next steps
* [Zoriana Solodukha, Veronica] Create Email Design: Create required styles design for the registration flow emails.
* [Danylo Boiko] Merge PR: Merge the necessary pull request for data synchronization.
* [Danylo Boiko] Resynchronize Data: Resynchronize all products and data within Medusa; process starts today, finishes tomorrow morning.
* [The group] Test Existing Login: Test login flow for existing users, like Eugene, after full data synchronization completes.
* [Artem Ugolkov, Vlad] Modify Data Flow: Recreate and modify the registration flow to send medical intake data to Impetus.
* [Artem Ugolkov, Vlad] Update HubSpot Status: Send a checkmark status to HubSpot confirming the intake form is filled.
* Provide Webhook: Provide the endpoint or webhook to Artem for medical intake data transmission.
* [Christian Leonard Roman] Coordinate Timeline: Coordinate with teams regarding the remaining project item timelines.
* [Christian Leonard Roman] Coordinate Handovers: Coordinate handover sessions between Shahbaz and Elguja.

12qUlyMxB8DInkW16ixJf3CQ6i7-m-R3otbONA4L.title: R&D Backlog Refinement
12qUlyMxB8DInkW16ixJf3CQ6i7-m-R3otbONA4L.summary:
Integration review confirmed DiRX and ScriptSure production readiness with discussions about the cancellation behavior of the ScriptSure IFrame and the necessary ID.me integration.

Pharmacy API Integration Status
DiRX, Hollandale, and ScriptSure API integrations are complete and in production, with Brooksville and Valiant channels scheduled for setup today. RXC integration is in production but requires follow-up because their API is not providing necessary order updates to the job.

IFrame Submission Status Debate
The team debated the correct order status when a user closes the ScriptSure IFrame after submitting an order, determining that closing the IFrame should not cancel a submitted order. The ID.me integration is required for provider onboarding and must be configurable based on the business unit and ScriptSure routing.

Future Features and Integration
The team confirmed the need for a Represcribe feature on the patient profile and a feature allowing administrators to upload pharmacy dosing guidelines for provider reference during prescribing. User registration will classify a person as a lead, and they will only be created as a patient in Impetus One after completing the medical intake form.




Next steps
* [Rizzi Ann Gorospe] Setup Pharmacy Hub: Setup channel in production for Pharmacy Hub integration.
* [Rizzi Ann Gorospe] Setup Channels: Set up production channels for Brooksville and Valiant pharmacies.
* [Rizzi Ann Gorospe] Disable Fireflies: Disable Fireflies in user settings as instructed by Rochelle Blaza.
* [The group] Setup IV Cure: Set up IV Cure integration in production.
* [The group] Setup Hometown: Set up Hometown via email in production environment.
* [Lorenz Lauraya] Check RXC Status: Check RXC API directly to verify if their order is still there. Update the corresponding ticket with findings.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Create Ticket: Create ticket regarding support for free form scripts in the patient prescription flow for ScriptSure.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Review Scenarios: List down all different scenarios for ScriptSure iFrame submission edge cases and take discussion offline with Adan Cruz.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Create Ticket: Create ticket for ID.me integration under settings, ensuring it is configurable based on business unit requirements.
* [Rizzi Ann Gorospe] Test Patient Sync: Test the patient demographic data synchronization flow from Impetus One to the ScriptSure account during prescription.
* [Rizzi Ann Gorospe] Perform Test Order: Execute 1 test order in production to verify data synchronization to the ScriptSure account.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Create Ticket: Create a ticket to build the Pharma API integration based on provided documentation.
* [The group] Prioritize Integration: Set integration of OK Capsule and Spot Health into Impetus One as low priority pending Samantha's final instruction.
* [Lorenz Lauraya] Implement Webhook Forwarding: Implement web hook forwarding solution for DRX and LifeFile tracking numbers from Impetus RX to Impetus One. Coordinate with Najib Boumekhiet for implementation details.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Share Guidelines: Share all current dosing guidelines samples including maximum file size and different accepted formats with Lorenz Lauraya.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Review Registration Workflow: Check with Konrad to determine if registration should immediately create a patient record in Impetus one, or wait for medical intake submission.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Schedule Meeting: Schedule new meeting next week. Discuss and finalize the design workflow.

1mYJzoqZXWHhqcougD8T-IirBz6UDwq4UB6XzP1s.title: Ironsail x Webugol Internal Marketing
1mYJzoqZXWHhqcougD8T-IirBz6UDwq4UB6XzP1s.summary:
Meeting participants configured the Mac Mini for Genus OS and demonstrated successful automated ad campaign generation.

Mac Mini Setup Completion
The team successfully established remote access for the Mac Mini and verified its 24 GB RAM capacity for local LLM operations. This hardware configuration enables the device to support autonomous Genus OS setup and embedding model deployment.

Merlin Bot Agent Configuration
Discussions focused on implementing API-based email management strategies and selecting high-performance models like Minimo V2 Pro for increased efficiency. The team prioritized using Google CLI to ensure comprehensive bot integration with Gmail and Calendar services.

Automated Campaign System Success
A demonstration confirmed that the KEX system generates high-quality, 95% complete ad campaigns via simple link inputs. This automation significantly optimizes workflow efficiency for peptide marketing and future client service scaling.




Next steps

1aa23SdW4zoNzublaYweyBAoDGi9vBGyBgiBxg9f.title: eCommerce Migration Weekly Update
1aa23SdW4zoNzublaYweyBAoDGi9vBGyBgiBxg9f.summary:
Front-end and back-end integration issues with product rendering were discussed, with subscription and loyalty program logic clarified for upcoming development.

Front-end Integration and Demo
The front-end is close to rendering all products, with previous 500 errors resolved, and the team reviewed the full checkout flow layout with placeholder data. The goal for next week is to render data for user authentication and product visibility, including fixes for logging issues and implementation of front-end tests.

Subscription and Loyalty Logic
Subscription prices remain fixed at the time of purchase, and any changes in membership tier only affect the price if a new subscription is created. It was agreed that the membership discount calculation should be implemented on the back-end (Medusa) side to manage discount logic.

Medical Intake Data Flow
Medical intake data should be sent directly to Hubspot and should not be stored in the Medusa database, with the same intake fields required for every separate product purchase. The team agreed on a strategy for routing this data, and it was decided that Artem Ugolkov's team will update the HubSpot contact lifecycle stage upon purchase.




Next steps
* [Elguja Nemsadze] Share Guide: Share guide with Artem explaining endpoints, authentication, and registration flow setup.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Create Test Task: Create a separate task for implementing frontend tests.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Create Log Task: Create a separate task for fixing frontend logs sent to the backend.
* [Elguja Nemsadze] Share Payment Guide: Share authorize.net payment flow guide with Artem for product purchase implementation.
* [The group] Update Subscription Pricing: Implement required functionality to automatically increase subscription prices when base product prices increase.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Create Webhook Subtask: Create a subtask in Jira for web hook implementation under the wellness blueprint delivery ticket.
* [Artem Ugolkov] Implement Hubspot Deals: Implement necessary synchronization for creating Hubspot deals upon order purchase completion.

1-BYOOZh-IOWoG1Pkf0Z1Rdqy1PCqIyIwu5DIlZL.title: Daily Standup Meeting
1-BYOOZh-IOWoG1Pkf0Z1Rdqy1PCqIyIwu5DIlZL.summary:
RX API issues dominated discussions, with e-commerce rollout and data migration validation also being key points.

RX API Issues Investigation
Nadjib Boumekhiet is investigating an RX generation issue in PracticeSync and will reach out to Conrad regarding the non-functional advanced ScriptSure API pop-up. The team is addressing deployment issues with the integrated RX API that prevented changes from reflecting in production.

E-commerce Rollout and Data Validation
Danylo Boiko completed the e-commerce back-end rollout for the production preview and is connecting it to the storefront in the same environment. Farhan Sheikh was assigned the crucial task of validating migrated data in the Medusa staging environment against WooCommerce, focusing on random users, order histories, products, and authentication endpoints.

Subscription and Integration Clarification
Elguja Nemsadze created a front-end guide for payments and subscriptions using Authorized.net tokens, which required modifying the migration script. The team clarified that Medusa should send registration and purchase events directly to HubSpot's endpoints for integration.




Next steps
* [Nadjib Boumekhiet] Investigate ScriptSure: Reach out to Konrad requesting issue instances. Investigate the advanced ScriptSure API module for PracticeSync.
* [Jhon Ray Angcon] Add Christian: Add Christian Leonard Roman access to the business unit deployment issue.
* [Danylo Boiko] Check Runtime: Check production runtime to find the issue preventing changes from reflecting.
* [Farhan Sheikh] Validate Migration: Check migrated user data, order history, and product listings against WordPress/WooCommerce. Validate Artem endpoints for authentication and card creation in Swagger.
* [Elguja Nemsadze] Share Subscription Details: Share the new authorized.net payment guide and the updated endpoints via MD file with Artem.
* [Elguja Nemsadze] Finish Loyalty Program: Work on the loyalty program and bonuses, aiming for completion by the end of this week.
* [Illia Tutko] Fix Tracking Number: Check the database and fix the issue where tracking information is not reflecting in Impetus RX.
* [Rizzi Ann Gorospe] Create Tracking Ticket: Create a new ticket for the order tracking number issue reported.
* [Shabbaz] Build HubSpot Integration: Work on the Medusa to HubSpot integration flow for customer registration and purchase events.

1wSNrlJ2rW257GwYoAvuVCZC_ofTXMf4VXj85gvu.title: Daily Standup Meeting
1wSNrlJ2rW257GwYoAvuVCZC_ofTXMf4VXj85gvu.summary:
Attendance uncertainty and ongoing API integrations for RX and United Pharmacy dominated the meeting with discussion of e-commerce environment setup and RX Genie functionality issues.

API Integration Updates Shared
Jhon Ray Angcon confirmed that the work for 2 business units was merged to staging for testing and received the correct API information for the RX API integration. Illia Tutko reported finding an issue with the United Pharmacy API’s 'create order' function, estimating the remaining integration work to take 3 days.

Technical Investigations and Setup
Nadjib Boumekhiet is investigating a non-functioning RX Genie feature which is part of PracticeSync, and Danylo Boiko completed the migration script rerun for the e-commerce preview environment. A new ticket was created for Impetus to assist with setting up Google Alerts on their service.

TPH Order Note Investigation
Rizzi Ann Gorospe created ticket VV1374 to investigate a missing 'Ironsail for a note' on TPH orders even with the autopopulated tag, which was observed on 3 sample TPH orders. The team decided to discuss offline replication efforts for the TPH order issue after a recent test order failed to process in dispense pro.
nextSteps: [Rizzi Ann Gorospe] Test Business: Test merged business unit code in staging environment.
[Rochelle Blaza] Share API: Update ticket and share API folder link for Jhon Ray integrations. Share folder link right now.
[Jhon Ray Angcon] Review API: Check the shared API folder and review documentation. Report required development time needed for integration.
[Illia Tutko] Deploy Fix: Push the pending issue fix related to United Pharmacy API to staging for testing.
[Illia Tutko] Update API: Work on the second integration ticket concerning creating 1 order per medication after fixing the pending issue.
[Rizzi Ann Gorospe] Check Tickets: Review tickets related to John Ray testing results. Check upcoming concerns from the Pharma team.
[Rochelle Blaza, Rizzi Ann Gorospe] Discuss TPH: Discuss the TPH order replication issue offline to determine the next steps.
[Christian Leonard Roman] Schedule 1:1: Check online availability for a one to one call with Illia. Connect via chat to confirm scheduling.

1Mek-TWa2EK3orJ1YTVj0eS65LMIYy-ApuwYZsxp.title: Weekly Catch-Up: Eugene & Philip
1Mek-TWa2EK3orJ1YTVj0eS65LMIYy-ApuwYZsxp.summary:
Drone industry growth and US-Ukraine alliance discussed alongside detailed plans for Medusa and Impetus One system integration.

AI-Driven Development Confidence
The team affirmed high confidence in using AI for projects like Medusa, noting that AI simplifies complex development, reducing reliance on traditional developers and drastically accelerating module completion. This new AI approach enables faster creation of tools and dashboards, with skepticism about AI adoption reported to be vanishing among team members.

Medusa and Impetus One Integration
A critical decision was made to integrate Medusa and Impetus One, aiming for an MVP release in April and full integration by May, replacing older systems like N8N. The new structure will feature Impetus One as the API-first backend for the patient portal, handling user authentication and medical data via seamless front-end calls.
nextSteps: [Philip D'Agostino] Withhold Bonus: Do not pay Greg the exit bonus until he delivers the project scope list and documentation.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Check HubSpot: Ask about the current status of the HubSpot migration to confirm data integrity.
[Eugene Ugolkov] Consult Valerie: Ask Valerie about the new Figma tool that transforms website designs into mobile applications.
[Philip D'Agostino] Plan Integration: Schedule a foundational meeting with Artem to integrate the Medusa and Impetus One platforms.
[Andrew] Invite Eugene: Invite Eugene Ugolkov to the next meeting to discuss marketing strategy and operational plan execution.
[Philip D'Agostino] Schedule Meeting: Schedule a meeting with Artem to start connecting. Aim for next week or this week for the connection kick-off.

1ymRGceoE4EvHIxWrTVYM3akjKqVHmTQC8wKHqrx.title: 30 min with Philip (Matt Budeir)
1ymRGceoE4EvHIxWrTVYM3akjKqVHmTQC8wKHqrx.summary:
Ironsail seeks hardware procurement for a private data center prototype with a focus on GPU sourcing and architecture support.

Data Center Prototype Scope
Ironsail is building a private data center prototype to serve its internal AI token distribution, initially requiring less than 5 kilowatts of power but planning to scale to 30 megawatts. The prototype requires approximately 2 terabytes of RAM to serve large language models to around 20 concurrent users before securing further investment.

GPU Hardware Procurement Strategy
GPU allocation for the newer Reuben MBL8 is challenging due to hyperscaler priority, leading Ironsail to consider starting with H100s or waiting if stacking is impossible. Ironsail has an initial budget of approximately $100,000 for the setup, and a reseller is required since direct business is limited to larger enterprises.

Architecture Support and Compliance
An Nvidia expert will assist Ironsail with system architecture and bill of materials identification, suggesting H-series GPUs for better cost efficiency than B-series. The software development entity, Ironsail LLC, will be the buyer, and compliance vetting regarding GPU export and commercial address is required.




Next steps
* [Imad Budeir] Find Reseller Partner: Find proper channel partner for purchasing; they must handle business with smaller enterprises.
* [Imad Budeir] Schedule Architect Call: Schedule follow-up call with Nvidia team; qualify requirements, determine Bill of Materials (BOM).
* [Imad Budeir] Email SGX Pricing: Send email detailing SGX tower station pricing information.
* [Imad Budeir] Send NDA: Send electronic NDA to Ironsail LLC; ensure completion before technical discussions.

1RD8Sj5mG6-K0uxnT_y9VOBWedIFPACYv9mBI-G7.title: Weekly Senior Leadership Standup
1RD8Sj5mG6-K0uxnT_y9VOBWedIFPACYv9mBI-G7.summary:
Regulatory changes for 14 innovative wellness peptides were discussed, with a resulting decision to launch preparatory email campaigns and clarify founder roles.

Category 1 Peptide Announcement
Intel suggested the Category 2 to Category 1 announcement is scheduled for Wednesday, April 8th, at 10:00 a.m., opening 14 hidden peptides for both pharma and vitality. The team decided to prepare for the announcement by making the product category switch easy and removing Rat True Tide from the transition list.

Pre-Announcement Strategy and Messaging
The team decided to execute an email campaign before the announcement with messaging like “big news is coming,” focusing on peptides and anticipation of good news. To maximize visibility, newly available Category 1 peptides will be moved from innovative wellness to categories like “hot new peptides” and their natural placement, such as weight loss.

Defining Founders' Roles and Equity
The 80/20 term sheet clause was clarified to reflect the founders' executive roles and focus on contribution and strategic direction rather than time commitment. It was confirmed that due diligence is complete, and initial funding is expected within 10 days, with the foundational cap table for vitality needing separation from Valhalla Holdings.




Next steps
* [Konrad Arciszewski] Plan Re-categorization: Write attack plan for April 8th category change, including website redesign and email campaigning preparation.
* [Konrad Arciszewski] Execute Email Campaign: Send initial big news email Monday. Prepare follow-up messaging for Wednesday/Thursday post-announcement, focusing on peptide release.
* [Konrad Arciszewski] Update Categories: Remove newly available peptides from Innovative Wellness. Create a Hot New Peptides category and ensure items disseminate into natural respective categories.
* [Samantha D'Agostino] Define Executive Roles: Define Philip and Samantha involvement for the actual contract with Marzen. Focus on strategic contribution and output rather than time commitment.
* [Samantha D'Agostino] Analyze Contribution: Use Gemini tool to review past 3 months of Vitality related conversations and emails to define current role and contribution played.
* [Philip D'Agostino] Modify Agreement: Change the Valhalla Holdings operating agreement. Remove stake held by individuals who breached contract.
* [Philip D'Agostino] Notify Stake Removal: Draft and send official letter notifying parties in Valhalla Holdings that their stake has been removed due to breach of contract.

1HMqI4ImtWHHxnbHEW3dXDG-6BewwhbWvwN-skRu.title: Daily Standup Meeting
1HMqI4ImtWHHxnbHEW3dXDG-6BewwhbWvwN-skRu.summary:
Drug banner display finalized for controlled drugs and ScriptSure drug warning persistence confirmed, with signature capture fix deployment ready for production testing.

Controlled Drug Banner Logic
The team decided that the high-display ScriptSure banner must only show for controlled drugs and remain hidden for non-controlled prescriptions. This decision ensures that non-controlled drugs sent directly to the pharmacy do not trigger drug warning popups.

Development Fixes and Updates
A fix for signature capture (VV 1390) was confirmed working in staging and is ready for production deployment. The accepted criteria for ticket 1372 were added to ensure consistency for multiple medications.

Transition and Testing Priorities
Loyalty system development is nearly complete, requiring a full handover to Shabbaz tomorrow, focusing on bug fixing and testing. The team decided that Shabbaz's immediate priority must be testing the Authorized.net payment implementation and the subscription cron job logic within Medusa.




Next steps
* [Jhon Ray Angcon] Hide Banner: Figure out how to hide the display banner for non-controlled drugs.
* [Illia Tutko] Re-route South Lake: Set up South Lake routing back to ScriptSure. Remove South Lake from UPA routing.
* [Pharma Team] Resend Orders: Reach out to the provider to resend South Lake orders made on March 27.
* [Danylo Boiko] Add Vault Roles: Add roles to all team members on Vault setup.
* [Danylo Boiko] Upgrade Dependencies: Upgrade vulnerable dependencies to fix Impetus pipelines.
* [Rizzi Ann Gorospe] Regression Test: Conduct a general regression test after Danylo deploys pipeline changes to staging.
* [Nadjib Boumekhiet] Investigate Tracking: Look into ticket VB1389 regarding tracking numbers not displaying Nimbus RX.
* [Nadjib Boumekhiet] Contact DRX: Reach out to DRX regarding the missing tracking information.
* [Nadjib Boumekhiet] Prepare Code Snippet: Show a code snippet to Agile proving that the tracking functionality is working.
* [Elguja Nemsadze] Handover Tasks: Conduct a transition/handover of tasks to Shabbaz, focusing on development wrap-up today.
* [Shabbaz] Test Payment: Focus on payment integration, testing Authorized.net consumption in Medusa.
* [Shabbaz] Test Subscription: Focus on testing the subscription logic and cron job functionality moved to Medusa.
* [Christian Leonard Roman] Connect Shabbaz: Connect with Shabbaz to discuss shifting his focus to payment and subscription testing.

1_vD0eHTv8LOXAcTPevPQtw-MLU2CLAelgtiVJlH.title: Weekly Catch-Up: Eugene & Philip
1_vD0eHTv8LOXAcTPevPQtw-MLU2CLAelgtiVJlH.summary:
Contract formalization for Valhalla Vitality with focus on conflict of interest and legal review, with discussion regarding KEX's successful beta marketing and quantum computing security concerns.

Contract Formalization and Review
Contract creation for Valhalla Vitality was necessary to address investor confusion about services and mitigate risk. The marketing contract and a software development contract need legal review to support the future-facing plan and pricing.

Software Delivery and AI Development
Software deliverables for Valhalla Vitality include a $700,000 mobile app by January and e-commerce migration to Medusa by June 1st. AI-driven development is leveraging Gemini Pro and Claude to build architecture and generate high-quality code, leading to fewer mistakes.

KEX Marketing and Operations
KEX marketing saw positive early results with 9 signups after blog launch, and the local SEO module is releasing in 10 days. The standardized service pipeline for all 10 clients will use a single project manager and Jira for tracking tasks.




Next steps
* [Philip D'Agostino] Draft Software Contract: Create the new contract for Valhalla Vitality software development services. Include front-end, Medusa, mobile app development, migration, and hosting details.
* [Eugene Ugolkov] Adapt Marketing Contract: Adapt existing service contract template for Valhalla Vitality marketing services. Ensure document reflects current investment plan and pricing based on the plan.
* [Dimma] Create API Case: Create a use case document for the API team justifying increased API limits. Explain the necessity for higher limits prior to release, due to large client base and daily data renewal needs.
* [Eugene Ugolkov] Follow up Email Issue: Ask Andrew about the status of fixing the 2 email issues requested by Shahad. Determine the cause and ensure immediate resolution today.
* [Eugene Ugolkov] Prepare Genus OS Install: Download the Genus OS git repository link to local using Claude Code before Thursday. Open a new session and navigate to the directory ready for installation.
* [Philip D'Agostino] Establish PM Process: Instruct Relle and Christian regarding new client management process, emphasizing Jira ticket creation, timely task closure, required client follow-up.
* [Eugene Ugolkov] Schedule Meeting: Meet with Relle and Christian to discuss their involvement and awareness regarding current and new clients managed.
* [Philip D'Agostino] Draft Contract: Complete work on the Valhalla Vitality client contract details.
* [Eugene Ugolkov] Review and Distribute Contract: Work on the contract documentation; send final version to Philip D'Agostino and Sam for review.
* [Philip D'Agostino] Contact Dr. Ken: Follow up with Dr. Ken; instruct him to contact Eugene Ugolkov regarding connecting with Diana for project information.
* [Eugene Ugolkov] Update Client Spreadsheet: Add Christian to client spreadsheet after outreach.

1SrSZjxJhbURf_GrL6hGiOLxPwPKPUUAAr_dUmUm.title: Daily Standup Meeting
1SrSZjxJhbURf_GrL6hGiOLxPwPKPUUAAr_dUmUm.summary:
Team updates covered API integrations and infrastructure changes, with immediate prioritization given to fixing the critical Hollandale signature issue.

Team Updates and API Progress
Development work included pushing the RX API integration to staging, merging quantity units to production, and completing 50% of the rewards management module. Infrastructure updates focused on Devit file server completion and the implementation of Single Sign-On for Vault to allow leads to manage environment variables.

Diagnosis of Hollandale Signature
The critical issue of missing signatures for controlled substances in Hollandale prescriptions was discussed and diagnosed as a failure to capture the most recent signature after a doctor signs. Rizzi Ann Gorospe reported finding a recent controlled substance order that did contain a signature, suggesting the issue might be intermittent.

Immediate Prioritization of Fix
The Hollandale signature issue was designated as the top priority, overriding Ilia Tutko’s current task on the United Pharmacy API ticket due to order rejection and delays. A South Lake order status failure was identified as a credential problem, with Rochelle Blaza taking ownership to confirm credentials with Anton.




Next steps
* [Rizzi Ann Gorospe] Test Integrations: Test the RX API integration and verify the add quantity units feature in production after deployment.
* [Christian Leonard Roman] Connect Team Members: Connect Eden and Vijay to discuss required help with ScriptSure assistance.
* [Illia Tutko] Fix Signature Bug: Prioritize and fix the Halenale missing signature issue immediately. This is critical because orders are currently being rejected.
* [Illia Tutko] Add PDF View: Implement action for viewing PDF in the relevant prescription page while fixing the signature bug.
* [Rizzi Ann Gorospe] Document Intermittency: Screenshot the working controlled substance order from David Wiggins. Input the details into the relevant ticket for investigation by Illia.
* [Illia Tutko] Send Credentials: Send a screenshot of the current South Lake credential settings to Rochelle Blaza.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Validate Credentials: Validate the South Lake credential settings for ticket 1388 against the document and email provided by the pharmacy.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Inform Anton: Email Anton regarding the API error related to South Lake credentials if validation confirms they are incorrect.

1USHehuvS1oAF_wFHW2n3HwtGFAve7hNMtqwNFV-.title: Meeting with Philip
1USHehuvS1oAF_wFHW2n3HwtGFAve7hNMtqwNFV-.summary:
Team transition efforts finalized with codebase handover, and project timelines established, with a need for formalized client support processes via streamlined ticketing.

Team Transition and Codebase
The team transition is concluding with the handover of the codebase and Ilgusha completing the loyalty and rewards module for Shahbaz. Shahbaz is now focused on understanding and testing the Medusa endpoints created by Ilgusha, with a proposal to begin integrating Medusa with Impetus 1 for data transmission.

Impetus 1 Rollout Timelines
Timelines were set for Valhalla Vitality PracticeSync functionality by May, with a full migration off Impetus RX by June, and Impetus RX onboarding new clients onto Impetus 1 in parallel by May. Self-registration and billing modules were identified as high-impact features needed for Ironsail Pharma to automate self-service onboarding.

Formalizing Client Support Workflow
A critical decision was made to formalize client support, routing all requests through Christian and Rochelle Blaza via a general support email linked to Jira for automatic ticket creation. The technical team structure was defined, placing Shahbaz and Loren in production while reserving Muhammad and Adan for the R&D team.




Next steps
* [Rochelle Blaza] Test 2FA: Complete testing of two-factor authentication piece this week.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Contact HR: Check with HR regarding Muhammad status and team addition readiness.
* [Philip D'Agostino] Resolve OpenAI: Figure out and resolve OpenAI account access or permissions issues.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Update RX Genie: Check with the team regarding the status of RX Genie/OpenAI API error fix.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Setup Support: Create generic support email address and link it to Jira for automatic ticket creation.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Discuss Support: Discuss new support email details and routing plan with Christian.
* [Philip D'Agostino] Coordinate Thrive: Instruct Danylo to connect the lovable project to the GIT repository for Thrive automatic deployments.
* [Rochelle Blaza] Automate Testing: Pair Farhan and Rizie to automate testing processes and tasks.

1ZJ9uQETG3aMOEmHkJCLNFighWLrhKGmxg5QQ365.title: Daily Standup Meeting
1ZJ9uQETG3aMOEmHkJCLNFighWLrhKGmxg5QQ365.summary:
The standup confirmed no blockers across teams for RXC and API integration, data validation, and Medusa loyalty implementation.

RXC API Integration Nears Completion
The RXC and API integration is nearly complete, with current work focusing on adapting PDF generation to support multiple drugs per prescription, scheduled for staging deployment by end-of-day after testing. Troubleshooting and automation tasks are planned, with Medusa data validation utilizing APIs and testing currently underway.

Medusa Loyalty Functionality Progresses
Loyalty functionality implementation in Medusa is progressing, specifically working on loyalty parity with WooCommerce without encountering any blockers. A request was made to update the complete knowledge base of the Astrid chatboard to ensure consistent answers.

UPA Development and Order Issues
Development on UPA continues, focusing on the input side to allow for creating one prescription with multiple medicines. Testing support included verifying the prescription PDF enhancement and addressing a failed South Lake order by creating and assigning a ticket to Illia.




Next steps
* [Christian Leonard Roman] Get Updates: Talk to absent team members offline regarding their project updates.
* [Nadjib Boumekhiet] Meet Farhan: Schedule meeting with Farhan to perform necessary automation work.
* [Shahbaz Hussain] Task Handover: Connect with Elguja concerning tasks requiring handover coordination.
* [Illia Tutko] Resolve Order: Work on the assigned ticket regarding the failed South Lake order status.
* [Rizzi Ann Gorospe] Coordinate Testing: Coordinate with Illia regarding tickets needing testing support.

1zO5uORf6WhHbeJheHatmv9pVaeqjs7wDupd7Bfh.title: Philip & Christian – 1:1 Intro Touch Base
1zO5uORf6WhHbeJheHatmv9pVaeqjs7wDupd7Bfh.summary:
The discussion centered on resolving dual stakeholder role inefficiencies by establishing defined processes via post-mortems and backlog refinement.

Dual Stakeholder Role Issues
Issues arise from the Chief Scientist having a dual stakeholder role, leading to requests for basic support that should be handled by a dedicated support team. Two post-mortems were requested to analyze recent improper escalations concerning Valhalla Vitality/RX Genie API access and a Kaduceus Google Workspace issue.

Improved Delegation and Process
The organization needs a defined process for delegation, escalation, and ticket closure, managed through Christian Roman and Rochelle, to ensure proper resource utilization. A critical decision was made to establish a system for documenting client contracts, services, and responsibilities, which will allow for identifying service gaps and necessary recruitment.

Streamlining Workflow and Priorities
Workflow will be streamlined by merging the separate daily standups into 1 combined meeting and implementing a backlog refinement session for project prioritization. This process will allow the Chief Scientist to focus on new prototyping while the team handles production needs and consults client stakeholders.




Next steps
* [Christian Leonard Roman] Conduct Postmortem: Troubleshoot Google Workspace issue for Kaduceus and RX Genie issue for Valhalla Vitality. Analyze occurred problems to structure a plan for future improvement.
* [Christian Leonard Roman, Rochelle] Create Delegation Plan: Develop suggestions for delegating tasks, defining escalation paths, and closing support tickets. Implement a clear process for handling client requests.
* [Christian Leonard Roman] Document Client Contracts: Define and record scope of work for client services, including exact line items and payment status. Identify the responsible team members for each service provided to clients like Valhalla Vitality and Kaduceus.
* [Christian Leonard Roman, Rochelle] Establish Backlog Process: Implement a Backlog Refinement session structure. Use the session to prioritize projects and discuss feature production readiness and client needs.
* [Philip D'Agostino] Contact Gary: Call Gary regarding potential new project work.
* [Christian Leonard Roman] Prepare Findings: Formulate initial ideas regarding the postmortem issues for the meeting tomorrow.

13C5N_jIM4HFCQ0mxNZgPtBdRpJZC8DVh65litOP.title: V/I Same Page Meetings
13C5N_jIM4HFCQ0mxNZgPtBdRpJZC8DVh65litOP.summary:
The meeting featured a retreat debrief, a discussion on travel logistics, and updates on due diligence for technology investments and development roadmaps.

Technology Investment Due Diligence
Due diligence catch-up is expected to finalize by the following day. A monthly budget of $30,000 to $35,000 is allocated for technology investments, covering e-commerce rollout, Impetus 1 upgrades, and the eventual app rollout.

Technology Roadmap Defined
A 3-step technology roadmap was outlined, starting with modernizing the technology stack, followed by upgrading features, and then rolling all features into a mobile application. The target for the full tech stack rollout and update is June 1st, feature upgrades by October, and the mobile app launch by January.

Operational Issues and Gaps
Two operational issues highlighted internal process gaps and the need for a services matrix and incident reporting to improve delegation and communication. The primary decision was that a dedicated technology operations manager is crucial for Valhalla Vitality to manage day-to-day business services and reduce reliance on the CEO for system issues.




Next steps
* [Philip D'Agostino] Submit Roadmap: Send 3-line note to Mazen outlining technology roadmap and specific dates.
* [Philip D'Agostino] Review Incidents: Meet with Christian to conduct an incident report review. Investigate Google Workspace and RX Genie API issues.
* [Christian] Develop Matrix: Build a services matrix detailing ownership and responsibilities.
* [Daniel McCarthy] Define Role: Draft high-level profile for the Technology Operations person role. Share profile with Philip and Jennifer for input.
* [Daniel McCarthy] Consult Mazin: Discuss necessity of an IT leadership role for Valhalla Vitality with Mazen.

1JnFgcdHBg-OinQ7DzW5tUUnbz8T1WAEUPdCjZNC.title: V/I Same Page Meetings
1JnFgcdHBg-OinQ7DzW5tUUnbz8T1WAEUPdCjZNC.summary:
Initial travel updates transitioned to an AI-first product development workshop, with structuring discussions for project management roles and team operations.

Workshop Planned for New Team
A 1-1.5 hour workshop focusing on the top-down building sculpting philosophy for AI first product development was proposed and agreed upon. Daniel McCarthy will coordinate this session for mid-April after the Grenada trip to allow new team members time to settle in.

New Project Manager Onboard
Christian Roman started as the new Project Manager, and Daniel McCarthy agreed to coach Christian and assist Rochelle with the process. The group decided that Christian should take the lead on managing client needs and delivery for clients such as Kaduceus and Thrive RX.

Clarify Role and Standup Structure
A critical issue regarding Christian Roman's 'Project Coordinator' title was raised and needs correction, emphasizing the need for an experienced Project Manager leader. The operational structure was defined, stipulating that Christian and Rochelle should run daily standups, removing this operational task from Philip D’Agostino’s responsibilities.
nextSteps: [Daniel McCarthy, Jennifer, Sandra] Plan AI Workshop: Organize a 1 to 1.5 hour AI first product development workshop for Philip’s team when he returns. Use the written document to create the session content.
[Daniel McCarthy] Coach New PM: Hold a coaching session with new Project Manager Christian Roman. Establish a successful working method for the role and the R&D team.
[Daniel McCarthy] Confirm PM Title: Write Jennifer, Samantha, Sandra, and Rochelle regarding Christian Romans incorrect Project Coordinator title. Push back to confirm Project Manager title.
[Daniel McCarthy] Prepare Litigation Summary: Create a simple summary of the Revive litigation. Insert this summary into the due diligence book.
